html:
Exclusive to:

The Potter's Wheel is a Crafting Station in Don't Starve Together, introduced in A New Reign. It requires 2 Cut Stone, 2 Boards and 4 Twigs to build, and a Science Machine to prototype. It allows players to access the Sculptures Filter and build many different sculptures, mostly based on Chess Pieces and Giants. Some of these sculptures are only available to craft during their associated seasonal Event.

Usage

The Bubble Pipe Carving and Carved Hornucopia sculptures are available near any Potter's Wheel, while the others must be unlocked by giving the Potter's Wheel the respective Sketches, which will be returned if the Wheel is hammered.

After being placed, a Potter's Wheel requires Marble, Cut Stone, or a Moon Shard to be placed on top it to work. After the chosen material is placed, the player can turn it into the chosen sculpture with 2 Rocks. Based on the material used, the sculpture will be white if Marble was used, black if Cut Stone was used, and a translucent green if a Moon Shard was used. After the sculpture is made, players can pick it up and carry it similar to Suspicious Marble.

Skins

Main article: Belongings

Woven - Elegant
Gothic Collection
Gothic Artificer's Table
Sculpt intricate stonework to your heart's content.
Woven - Elegant
Swamp Collection
Primitive Potter's Wheel
A stone sculting swampy spin-off.

Trivia

  • The Potter's Wheel was introduced in the Arts and Crafts update.

Assets

Primitive Potter's Wheel

  • Build
  • Place
  • Hit
  • Loop
  • Burnt

Gallery

  • Potter's Wheel as seen in the poster for the Arts and Crafts update.
  • A burnt Potter's Wheel.
  • The icon for a sculpting material on the Potter's Wheel.

"sculptingtable"
“We can make stone sculptures with this.”
–Wilson
“Hm... I could make things that DON'T burn?”
–Willow
“Is table for rocks!”
–Wolfgang
“That looks... f-fun...”
–Wendy
“STONE IS SLIGHTLY LESS INFERIOR THAN FLESH”
–WX-78
“A method of ceramic production. We somehow use it for sculpture.”
–Wickerbottom
“Oughta put a block of cut stone up there.”
–Woodie
“A block of marble might suffice.”
–Maxwell
“A transförmative altar för the stöne göds.”
–Wigfrid
“I always wanted pottery lessons!”
–Webber
“Just need some stone to get cooking.”
–Warly
“No rock yet”
–Wormwood
“Not bad for a handmade table.”
–Winona
“Artist's block. Hyuyu!”
–Wortox
“Make things outta rocks!”
–Wurt
“Time for some arts and crafts.”
–Walter
“I've got more pressing things to do than arts and crafts.”
–Wanda

After the chosen material is placed, the player can turn it into the chosen sculpture with 2 [[Rocks]]. Based on the material used, the sculpture will be white if Marble was used, black if Cut Stone was used, and a translucent green if a Moon Shard was used. After the sculpture is made, players can pick it up and carry it similar to [[Suspicious Marble]]. == [[File:Skins Icon.png|32px|link=]] Skins == {{Main|Belongings#Structure Skins|Belongings}}
